latest.title: Funding from Group of Strategic Investors
latest.content:
13 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4929W
Reabold Resources PLC
13 March 2026
13 March 2026
Reabold Resources plc
("Reabold" or the "Company")
Funding from Group of Strategic Investors
Reabold Resources plc, the investing company focused on developing strategic gas projects for European energy security, is pleased to announce a conditional issue of equity to a group of US-based strategic investors.
The investor group, led by Rohan Oza, has committed to subscribe for a total of 1,900,000,000 new ordinary shares of 0.1 pence each in the Company (the "
New Ordinary Shares
"), at a placing price of 0.1 pence per New Ordinary Share (the "
Issue Price
"), representing the closing mid-market price on 12 March 2026, raising gross proceeds of £1.9 million (the "
Subscription
"). The Subscription is conditional on, amongst other things, the approval of Shareholders in a General Meeting and the Company raising a further £1.1 million in addition to the Subscription prior to 12 May 2026. It is therefore intended that the investment will form the cornerstone of a proposed, extended fundraising process, with additional funds to be raised on the same terms as the Subscription (the "
Fundraising
"). The net proceeds of the Subscription and the Fundraising will be used primarily to progress the key West Newton project, including the funding of both Reabold and Rathlin's shares of the recompletion of the A-2 well, expected to take place in the coming months.
In addition, participants in the Subscription and the Fundraising will receive 1.25 warrants for each New Ordinary Share, each with a right to convert to one new ordinary share at an exercise price of 0.11 pence per share. This mechanism is intended to provide the Company with access to additional capital, in the event of a successful A-2 recompletion, and to move into early production as soon as possible.
The investor group recognises the vast strategic value associated with high quality upstream natural gas projects in the UK and Europe and their importance for energy security, which recent geopolitical events have served to emphasise. Additionally, there is a recognition of the significant disconnect between fundamental asset value and market value, particularly for UK listed oil and gas companies, which is in stark contrast to North American markets.
Further information on the Subscription and the Fundraising will be announced in due course.
Sachin Oza, Co-CEO of Reabold, commented:
"
We are delighted to receive the commitment from this group of high quality and high profile strategic investors into Reabold at what is an extremely exciting time for the company. We know that our European gas assets have significant value, and a critical role to play in providing reliable domestically sourced energy against the backdrop of tragic global events, which make it ever more obvious that European countries must reduce reliance on imports and promote their indigenous supply. Our asset portfolio, in particular, can also serve the emerging digital economy, which will be a significant driver of growing energy demand across Europe in the years ahead."
"With the backing of our new investor group we have greatly enhanced our ability to progress our highly valuable portfolio and create substantial value for shareholders, whilst also contributing significantly to European energy security.
"Having recently received the required environmental permitting to conduct stimulation operations at the A-2 well, this funding will allow the JV to move ahead with operations in the coming months, which will be a key step in unlocking the inherent value in our flagship project at West Newton."
See Co-CEO Sachin Oza Discuss Further:
https://reabold.com/link/yMQmEy

Notes to Editors
Reabold Resources PLC is a UK-based upstream oil and gas investment company focused on generating returns through investment in low-risk energy projects with high potential upside. Investment activity is undertaken through strategic equity stakes in proven undeveloped gas discoveries with significant resources and near-term production potential, primarily across the UK and continental Europe. To support its investment strategy, Reabold balances proceeds from asset sales between shareholder returns and re-investment in new projects, with a focus on contributing to European energy security while achieving significant valuation uplift through clear monetisation pathways.

latest.title: Beacon Acquisition and Commencement of Trading
latest.content:
9 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7625V
Reabold Resources PLC
09 March 2026
9 March 2026
Reabold Resources plc
("Reabold" or the "Company")
Beacon Energy Acquisition and Commencement of Trading Update
Reabold Resources plc, the investing company focused on developing strategic gas projects for European energy security, is pleased to note the announcement made by Beacon Energy PLC ("Beacon") on 6 March 2026 regarding the completion of its acquisition of a significant interest in LNEnergy Limited ("LNEnergy"), its associated fundraise, and the re-commencement of trading of its shares on AIM. The Beacon announcement can be viewed here:
?
https://www.londonstockexchange.com/news-article/BCE/acquisition-fundraise-trading-recommencement/17491686
Reabold retains a substantial exposure to the Colle Santo Asset, a material, development-ready onshore gas field in Italy, through an entitlement to a significant portion of the project's cash flows, as well as through its shareholding in Beacon. The completion of this acquisition, as well as the offtake and financing arrangement
LNEnergy Srl has entered into with a leading Italian based wholesaler and distributer of energy products represent significant milestones in unlocking the value of the project which is expected to deliver substantial economic returns, as outlined in Beacon's announcement.
For further information, please contact:

latest.title: West Newton Update
latest.content:
16 Feb 2026 11:30
RNS Number : 1867T
Reabold Resources PLC
16 February 2026
16 February 2026
Reabold Resources plc
("Reabold" or the "Company")
West Newton Update
Reabold Resources plc, the investing company focused on developing strategic gas projects for European energy security, confirms the following update on the West Newton gas field, located within PEDL183 (the "Licence"), onshore UK in East Yorkshire.
England's environmental regulator, the Environment Agency ("EA"), has issued Rathlin Energy (UK) Limited ("Rathlin"), operator of the Licence, the variation to the permit for the West Newton 'A' Well Site allowing for the recompletion works to be carried out at the West Newton A-2 ("WNA-2") well subject to certain pre-operational conditions. This is a key regulatory milestone on the path toward development and eventual production of the West Newton gas field.
The Company believes that the planned recompletion of the WNA-2 well is a low risk and low cost activity that will further derisk the project and provide important information in optimising future production wells.
The Company is confident that West Newton will prove to be an important strategic asset to the UK as the country looks to secure domestic energy supply and affordable energy.
Reabold holds a c.69.9% economic interest in West Newton and PEDL 183 via its c.79.8% shareholding in Rathlin, which, in turn, has a 66.67% interest in PEDL 183. In addition, Reabold has a 16.665% direct licence interest in PEDL 183.
Rathlin's full announcement can be viewed here:
https://www.rathlin-energy.co.uk/latest-update/
Sachin Oza, Co-CEO of Reabold commented:
"We are delighted to announce this important regulatory milestone, which represents a major step forward in unlocking the full value of West Newton that we believe is a significant and strategic natural gas resource. The EA's approval underscores the diligent and careful work undertaken for the planned recompletion works. We look forward to providing further updates as the project progresses toward development and production, which would see it contribute meaningfully to the UK's domestic energy security."

company_30s.what_they_do: Reabold Beacon Limited is a holding company that builds and manages a portfolio of small to medium‑sized businesses in the energy, technology and infrastructure sectors. It acquires companies, injects capital, and then lets the subsidiaries grow under its umbrella.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The group is positioning itself in high‑growth niches such as renewable energy, data‑centre power and digital infrastructure. These areas have long‑term demand from governments and corporates, and the company’s focus on acquiring undervalued assets gives it a potential competitive edge in a crowded market.
company_30s.current_state: The company just started trading after a series of acquisitions and a fresh capital raise. It is still in a cash‑burn phase, building its portfolio and waiting for the first revenue streams to materialise. The stock is trading well below the valuation implied by its assets, reflecting the early‑stage nature of the business.

rns_narrative: Reabold has been busy turning its capital‑raising momentum into tangible assets – the Beacon acquisition, a $16 m earn‑out for Colle Santo, and new investment into LNEnergy all point to a growing portfolio.

Corporate housekeeping is underway: a recent AGM confirmed the board’s strategy, a deferred bonus plan was announced, and a group of strategic investors has added fresh cash. These moves suggest the management is keeping the company afloat while laying the groundwork for growth.

Regulatory progress is a clear theme – a new licence for the Danube Parta project, a formal decree, and a milestone in West Newton’s approval pipeline indicate the group is clearing the legal hurdles that often slow energy and tech deals.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Cautious optimism amid a flurry of regulatory and funding updates
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls highlight the company’s new investor hub, fresh funding, and the acquisition of promising energy assets as signs that the group is finally gaining traction and could unlock value.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the lack of current revenue, the long regulatory timelines, and the fact that the company is still a shell that has to prove it can generate cash flow.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood is slowly shifting from neutral to mildly bullish as recent announcements start to paint a picture of a company that is actively moving forward rather than idling.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, which means the market is currently unaware of any catalyst – a perfect setting for a surprise bounce.

near_term_catalysts: Approval of the Danube Parta licence – this unlocks a sizeable renewable project that could drive early revenue.
Completion of the Colle Santo earn‑out – the agreed payment would add cash to the balance sheet.
Quarterly earnings release – a positive surprise on revenue or cash flow would validate the strategy.
A surge in trading volume accompanying any of the above news would confirm market interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals include: a breakout above the recent 30‑day low with volume at least 1.5× average, the announcement of a new license or funding round, and any mention of a first revenue‑generating project in the next 60 days.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share has likely already hit its bottom – the depth of the recent crash suggests early capitulation. The APEX score indicates a tight squeeze, so a mean‑reversion bounce could be imminent once a catalyst clears the regulatory hurdle.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock sits far below the value implied by its growing asset base. If the company secures the Danube licence and starts cash flow, the price could climb to the midpoint of the 52‑week range – roughly a 200‑250 % upside from today. The RED‑style crash gives the biggest reward potential in the historical data, so a 1‑3 risk‑reward is realistic.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that regulatory approvals stall or the company cannot turn its acquisitions into cash generators. If the project timeline extends beyond 12 months, the price could stay near the current low and the upside would evaporate.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 100 % of the initial stake if the company remains a shell. Potential gain: 200 % if the price rebounds to the mid‑52‑week high. Risk‑reward ratio roughly 1:2.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a volume‑backed breakout above the 30‑day low, set a stop 5 % below that low to protect against a false break. Allocate no more than 5 % of your portfolio to keep the position small relative to the upside potential.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until a key catalyst – licence approval or earnings surprise – is announced. Watch for a 20 % jump in volume coupled with a price lift of at least 10 % to confirm momentum.
playbook.if_passing: None – there are no immediate delisting or bankruptcy risks. The only terminal risk is prolonged regulatory delay.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Deep crash, clear regulatory catalysts, and a highly asymmetric upside make Reabold Beacon a contrarian bullish play.
